title: hephaestus-beat7-reckoning
prompt:
Match the exact painterly cinematic concept-art style, palette, color, and rendering of the reference image — cool electric blue and violet shadow, warm gold automata, orange spark-light, volumetric haze, visible brushwork. Reproduce its look precisely; introduce no style not present in the reference.

Render ONE finished image: a 3x2 grid of six cinematic storyboard panels, thin black borders, narrow gutters, a number and short shot-type caption top-left of each. Editorial coverage of a SINGLE dramatic beat — THE RECKONING — in the forge; same Hephaestus, same gold automata, same concept-art look across all six.

STORY/SUBTEXT: The patron Kronos/Saturn — who devours the future the golden army harvests — has come. The line is halted. Hephaestus has hidden the one automaton whose eyes opened and met his; now he faces the consequence and loses his place, but keeps the one. Needed-back over needed; the cost of conscience; the patron's faceless, devouring authority. Dread hardening into quiet defiance, then bittersweet.

180-DEGREE RULE: axis between Hephaestus (screen LEFT, facing right) and the patron's looming dark mass (screen RIGHT); the camera never crosses it.

Panels:
1. WIDE — the forge gone still, the line of gold automata halted and darkening, a vast shadow falling across the ranks.
2. LOW ANGLE — the patron: a towering faceless silhouette of devouring stillness, screen-right, the gold ranks small beneath it.
3. MEDIUM — Hephaestus small before the shadow, screen-left, turned to face it, defiant and afraid.
4. INSERT — his hand reaching back behind him, shielding and hiding the one automaton from view.
5. CLOSE UP — the hidden automaton's serene gold face, eyes open, turned toward Hephaestus — the thing he protects.
6. FINAL — Hephaestus alone with the single automaton, the other ranks gone dark behind them; intimate and bittersweet, he has kept the one.

One continuous forge, cool blue-violet gloom deepened by the shadow, the red furnace column guttering.
